coredata.dc:title: Ultrasound-assisted Hydrothermal Renovation of LiCoO2 from the Cathode of Spent Lithium-ion Batteries
coredata.prism:copyright: Copyright © 2014 The Authors. Published by ESG. Published by Elsevier B.V.
coredata.prism:publisher: The Authors. Published by ESG. Published by Elsevier B.V.
coredata.dc:creator: Zhang, Zheming
He, Wenzhi
Li, Guangming
Xia, Jing
Hu, Huikang
Huang, Juwen
coredata.dc:description: LiCoO2 powders are separated from aluminum sheet of spent LiCoO2 electrodes and renovated by ultrasound radiation. LiCoO2 was obtained from electrode materials by burning off carbon and binder of the spent LiCoO2 electrodes at 500 °C for 1 h. Used LiCoO2 material was renovated by means of hydrothermal ultrasound radiation in a concentrated LiOH solution at 80°C, ultrasonic power 600W and ultrasonic radiation time for 6h. The experimental results show that the ultrasonic shearing effects produced hydroxyl radical which could help achieve the removing of Polyvinylidene fluoride (PVDF) , the renovated LiCoO2 had high degree of crystallinity, fine layered structure, good dispersion of particles and improved electrochemical cycle performance. The renovated LiCoO2 phase exhibits the first discharge capacity of 133.5mAh/g and the discharge capacity retention of 99.5 After 40 cycles which can reach electrochemical properties of commercial batteries.
coredata.dcterms:subject: spent lithium-ion batteries
LiCoO2
ultrasound radiation
Polyvinylidene fluoride

xocs:meta.xocs:articleinfo: rawtext articleinfo articletitlenorm authfirstinitialnorm authfirstsurnamenorm cid cids contenttype copyright crossmark dateloaded dateloadedtxt datesearch datesort dateupdated dco docsubtype doctype doi eid ewtransactionid hubeid indexeddate issfirst issn issnnorm issuelist itemstage itemtransactionid itemweight oauserlicense openaccess openarchive pg pgfirst pglast pii piinorm pubdateend pubdatestart pubdatetxt pubyr sortorder srctitle srctitlenorm srctype ssids alllist content oa subj tomb volfirst volissue volumelist webpdf webpdfpagecount yearnav affil articletitle auth authfirstini authfull authkeywords authlast primabst ref

xocs:funding.xocs:funding-agency-matched-string: National Natural Science Foundation of China
xocs:funding.xocs:funding-agency: National Natural Science Foundation of China

xocs:funding-list.xocs:funding-text: ACKNOWLEDGEMENT The authors gratefully acknowledge the financial support from the National Natural Science Foundation of China (Grant No. 51078286).

xocs:doc.xocs:rawtext: Ultrasound-assisted Hydrothermal Renovation of LiCoO2 from the Cathode of Spent Lithium-ion Batteries Zheming Zhang, 1 Wenzhi He, 1 hithwz@163.com Guangming Li, 1 Jing Xia, 1 Huikang Hu, 2 Juwen Huang, 1 1 State Key Laboratory of Pollution Control and Resource Reuse, School of Environmental Science and Engineering, Tongji University, Shanghai, China State Key Laboratory of Pollution Control and Resource Reuse, School of Environmental Science and Engineering Tongji University Shanghai China State Key Laboratory of Pollution Control and Resource Reuse, School of Environmental Science and Engineering, Tongji University, Shanghai, China 2 Department of Chemistry, Tongji University, Shanghai, China Department of Chemistry Tongji University Shanghai China Department of Chemistry, Tongji University, Shanghai, China LiCoO2 powders are separated from aluminum sheet of spent LiCoO2 electrodes and renovated by ultrasound radiation. LiCoO2 was obtained from electrode materials by burning off carbon and binder of the spent LiCoO2 electrodes at 500 Â°C for 1 h. Used LiCoO2 material was renovated by means of hydrothermal ultrasound radiation in a concentrated LiOH solution at 80Â°C, ultrasonic power 600W and ultrasonic radiation time for 6h. The experimental results show that the ultrasonic shearing effects produced hydroxyl radical which could help achieve the removing of Polyvinylidene fluoride (PVDF) , the renovated LiCoO2 had high degree of crystallinity, fine layered structure, good dispersion of particles and improved electrochemical cycle performance. The renovated LiCoO2 phase exhibits the first discharge capacity of 133.5mAh/g and the discharge capacity retention of 99.5 After 40 cycles which can reach electrochemical properties of commercial batteries. Keywords spent lithium-ion batteries LiCoO2 ultrasound radiation Polyvinylidene fluoride References 1. J.M. Tarascon, M Armand, Nature 414: (2001) 359-J.M. Tarascon and M Armand, Nature, 414 (2001) 359. 2. A. Vayrynen, J. Salminen, J Che, Thermodynamics 46: (2012) 80-A. Vayrynen, J. Salminen and J Che Thermodynamics, 46 (2012) 80. 3. A. Patil, V. Patil, D.W. Shin, Materials Res Bulletin 43: (2008) 1913-A. Patil, V. Patil and D.W. Shin, Materials Res Bulletin, 43 (2008) 1913. 4. M. Broussely, G. Archdale, J Power Sources 136: (2004) 386-M. Broussely and G. Archdale, J Power Sources, 136 (2004) 386. 5. Y.N. Dai, B. Yang, Y.C. Yao, Battery Bimonthly (China) 35: (2005) 193-Y.N. Dai, B. Yang and Y.C. Yao, Battery Bimonthly (China), 35 (2005) 193. 6. J.H. Li, P.X. Shi, Z.F. Wang, Chemosphere 77: (2009) 1132-J.H. Li, P.X. Shi, and Z.F. Wang, Chemosphere, 77 (2009) 1132. 7. D.I. Ra, K.S. Han, J Power Sources 163: (2006) 284-D.I. Ra and K.S. Han, J Power Sources, 163 (2006) 284. 8. J.M. Nan, D.M. Han, X.X. Zuo, J Power Sources 152: (2005) 278-J.M. Nan, D.M. Han and X.X. Nelson, M.A.O'Keefe, Nature Materials, 2 (2003) 464. 14. J.N. Reimers, J.R. Dahn, J Electrochem Soc 139: (1992) 2091-J.N. Reimers and J.R. Dahn, J Electrochem Soc, 139 (1992) 2091. 15. H. simple-head.ce:title: Ultrasound-assisted Hydrothermal Renovation of LiCoO2 from the Cathode of Spent Lithium-ion Batteries

ce:affiliation.ce:textfn: State Key Laboratory of Pollution Control and Resource Reuse, School of Environmental Science and Engineering, Tongji University, Shanghai, China

sa:affiliation.sa:organization: State Key Laboratory of Pollution Control and Resource Reuse, School of Environmental Science and Engineering

ce:affiliation.ce:source-text: State Key Laboratory of Pollution Control and Resource Reuse, School of Environmental Science and Engineering, Tongji University, Shanghai, China

ce:affiliation.ce:textfn: Department of Chemistry, Tongji University, Shanghai, China
ce:affiliation.ce:source-text: Department of Chemistry, Tongji University, Shanghai, China

ce:abstract-sec.ce:simple-para: LiCoO2 powders are separated from aluminum sheet of spent LiCoO2 electrodes and renovated by ultrasound radiation. LiCoO2 was obtained from electrode materials by burning off carbon and binder of the spent LiCoO2 electrodes at 500 °C for 1 h. Used LiCoO2 material was renovated by means of hydrothermal ultrasound radiation in a concentrated LiOH solution at 80°C, ultrasonic power 600W and ultrasonic radiation time for 6h. The experimental results show that the ultrasonic shearing effects produced hydroxyl radical which could help achieve the removing of Polyvinylidene fluoride (PVDF) , the renovated LiCoO2 had high degree of crystallinity, fine layered structure, good dispersion of particles and improved electrochemical cycle performance. The renovated LiCoO2 phase exhibits the first discharge capacity of 133.5mAh/g and the discharge capacity retention of 99.5 After 40 cycles which can reach electrochemical properties of commercial batteries.
